sourceMovie.title: Happy Death Day
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Happy Death Day works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Cabin in the Woods
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Happy Death Day clicked for you, The Cabin in the Woods (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Scream 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Happy Death Day clicked for you, Scream 2 (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Happy Death Day?
faq.answer: Try The Cabin in the Woods, Scream 2, The Lost Boys—each shares DNA with Happy Death Day while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Happy Death Day?
faq.answer: Happy Death Day (2017) is best described as Comedy, Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Happy Death Day part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Happy Death Day Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
